digest.component_or_area: v1.0 release pipeline and store-compatibility gate
propositions: Releases must be initiated from protected main via workflow_dispatch and publish only through an OIDC trusted-publishing pypi environment, eliminating any tag-triggered publish path so a hand-pushed v* tag cannot publish.

A v1.0 binary must read a committed store-compatibility record and fail closed on a present-but-incompatible or corrupt record across all mutation/publish paths, while treating an absent record as legacy version 0 (compatible) to preserve upgrade-in-place and rollback safety.

The published wheel currently bakes COMMIT=None and CI installs the source tree rather than the artifact, so the build must be done once and the exact hash-verified wheel/sdist tested and published with a correct non-null source commit.

Every release-time executable (actions, build tools, MCP publisher) must be pinned to immutable SHA/digest references and checkouts must not persist credentials, aligning release.yml with the stricter gerrit-verify.yaml convention.

Removed environment variables and TOML keys affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, or lifecycle policy must fail loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit rather than being silently ignored.
Version lockstep across the version input, pyproject.toml, and server.json must be asserted before build.
